quote: You have power over your mind, not outside events.
body:
You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.

The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.

Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.

Never let the future disturb you. You will meet it, if you have to, with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present.

quote: Make the best use of what is in your power.
body:
Make the best use of what is in your power, and take the rest as it happens. Some things are in our control and others not. Things in our control are opinion, pursuit, desire, aversion, and, in a word, whatever are our own actions.

Things not in our control are body, property, reputation, command, and, in one word, whatever are not our own actions. The things in our control are by nature free, unrestrained, unhindered.

Seek not that the things which happen should happen as you wish; but wish the things which happen to be as they are, and you will have a tranquil flow of life.

quote: We suffer more in imagination than in reality.
body:
We suffer more in imagination than in reality. Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.

It is not that I am brave, but that I know what is not worth fearing. While we are postponing, life speeds by.

Concentrate all your thoughts upon the work at hand. The sun's rays do not burn until brought to a focus.

Let us prepare our minds as if we had come to the very end of life. Let us postpone nothing. Let us balance life's books each day.

quote: Loss is nothing else but change, and change is nature's delight.
body:
Loss is nothing else but change, and change is nature's delight. Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, and do so with all your heart.

Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking. If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself, but to your estimate of it.

Never esteem anything as of advantage to you that will make you break your word or lose your self-respect. How much more grievous are the consequences of anger than the causes of it.

quote: No man is free who is not master of himself.
body:
No man is free who is not master of himself. First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do.

Men are disturbed not by the things which happen, but by the opinions about the things. When we are troubled or grieved, let us never blame anyone else, but ourselves.

If thy brother wrongs thee, remember not so much his wrong-doing, but more than ever that he is thy brother. It is not he that reviles you or strikes you, but your opinion that these things are insulting.

Character is revealed in every moment of choice. Every difficulty in life presents us with the opportunity to turn inward and to invoke our own submerged strengths.

quote: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a great deal of it.
body:
It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a great deal of it. Life is long enough, and a sufficiently generous amount has been given to us for the highest achievements if it were all well invested. But when it is wasted in heedless luxury and spent on no good activity, we are forced at last by death's final constraint to realize that it has passed away before we knew it was passing.

Recall it to your mind: how many different people have robbed you of life when you were not aware of what you were losing; how much was taken up in useless grief, foolish joy, greedy desire, the conversation of society; how little of yourself was left to you.

Postpone nothing. Balance life's books each day.

quote: Confine yourself to the present.
body:
Confine yourself to the present. When you arise in the morning, think of what a privilege it is to be alive, to think, to enjoy, to love.

Observe constantly that all things take place by change, and accustom yourself to consider that the nature of the universe loves nothing so much as to change the things which are. For the past, it is nothing. For the future, it is nothing yet. Only the present is yours.

Do not indulge in dreams of what you do not have, but count up the chief of the blessings you do have, and think how eagerly they would have been sought if you did not have them.

quote: Do not waste the remainder of your life in thoughts about other people.
body:
Do not waste the remainder of your life in thoughts about other people, except when you have some objective connected with the common good. Consider what is good, not who thinks what.

Everything harmonizes with me, which is harmonious to thee, O Universe. Nothing for me is too early or too late, which is in due time for thee. Everything is fruit to me which thy seasons bring, O Nature.

The first rule is to keep an untroubled spirit. The second is to look things in the face and know them for what they are.

quote: Virtue is the only thing that renders life happy.
body:
Virtue is the only thing that renders life happy, and no life is happy unless it is accompanied by virtue. Virtue is not just a matter of knowing the right thing to do; it is about doing it consistently, no matter the cost or the circumstances.

The happy man is one who has a sound body, a resourceful mind, and wisdom in his practice. He is one who fears nothing because he knows that fear is a tax paid by anticipation. He rejoices moderately in fortune and is not undone by misfortune.

Philosophy promises us above all else: common sense, humanity, and community. Cherish these bonds. They are the only ones that matter.

quote: Seek not that the things which happen should happen as you wish.
body:
Seek not that the things which happen should happen as you wish; but wish the things which happen to be as they are, and you will have a tranquil flow of life.

Never say about anything, I have lost it; but, I have returned it. Is your child dead? It has been returned. Is your wife dead? She has been returned. Is your estate taken away? Well, and is not that likewise returned? But he who took it away is a bad man. What difference is it to you who the giver assigns to take it back?

To make the best of what is in our power, and take the rest as it occurs. When you are offended at any man's fault, immediately turn to yourself and reflect in what like manner you do err yourself.

quote: Retire into yourself as much as you can.
body:
Retire into yourself as much as you can; associate with those who will make a better man of you. Welcome those whom you yourself can improve. The process is mutual; for men learn while they teach.

Let philosophy scrape off your own faults, rather than be a vehicle for abusing others. This is what I mean: withdraw into yourself, to the greatest possible extent. Mix with those who are likely to improve you. Let there be constant traffic between you and them.

One swallow does not make a spring, nor one fine day; and so too one day, or a short time, does not make a man blessed and happy.

quote: Begin the morning by saying to thyself: I shall meet with the busy-body, the ungrateful, arrogant, deceitful, envious, unsocial.
body:
Begin the morning by saying to thyself: I shall meet with the busy-body, the ungrateful, arrogant, deceitful, envious, unsocial. All these things happen to them by reason of their ignorance of what is good and evil.

But I who have seen the nature of the good that it is beautiful, and of the bad that it is ugly, and the nature of him who does wrong, that it is akin to me, not only of the same blood or seed, but sharing the same mind and the same portion of the divinity — I can neither be injured by any of them, for no one can fix on me what is ugly, nor can I be angry with my kinsman, nor hate him.

For we are made for co-operation, like feet, like hands, like eyelids, like the rows of the upper and lower teeth.

quote: People are not disturbed by things, but by the view they take of things.
body:
People are not disturbed by things, but by the view they take of things. When, therefore, we are hindered, or disturbed, or grieved, let us never blame someone else but ourselves — that is, our own judgments.

Do not seek to have events happen as you want them to, but instead want them to happen as they do happen, and your life will go well.

If you want to improve, be content to be thought foolish and stupid with regard to external things. Don't wish to seem to know anything; and if you appear to be somebody important to anyone, distrust yourself. For it is not easy at the same time to keep your prohairesis in a condition conformable to nature and also to pursue external things.

quote: Let right deeds be thy motive, not the fruit which comes from them.
body:
Let right deeds be thy motive, not the fruit which comes from them. And live in the action, labour! Make thine acts thy piety, casting all self aside, contemning gain and merit; so shall thine acts bring no bondage.

Never the spirit was born; the spirit shall cease to be never. Never was time it was not; End and Beginning are dreams.

It is better to do one's own duty, however imperfectly, than to assume the duties of another person, however successfully.

quote: The mind is restless and difficult to restrain, but it is subdued by practice.
body:
The mind is restless and difficult to restrain, but it is subdued by practice. For those whose minds are uncontrolled, reaching the highest state is very hard. But those whose minds are controlled, and who strive by the right means, it is possible.

A lamp does not flicker in a place where no wind blows; so it is with a yogi who controls his mind, intellect and self, being absorbed in the spirit within him.

Let each man raise the self by the self. Let him not suffer the self to be lowered. For the Self is the friend of the self, and also the Self is the enemy of the self.

quote: Act without expectation, without wasting time on what is past.
body:
Act without expectation, without wasting time on what is past, and with equanimity in both success and failure. Yoga is equanimity.

You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to not doing your duty.

As a fire is covered by smoke and a mirror is obscured by dust, as the embryo rests deep within the womb, wisdom is hidden by selfish desire. Wisdom is hidden by this never-satisfying fire of the senses, the eternal enemy of the wise.

The senses are said to be superior to the body; the mind is superior to the senses; reason is superior to the mind; and the Self is superior to reason.

quote: He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate to all — he is dear to Me.
body:
He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate to all, who is free from attachment and egoism, who is equal in pleasure and pain, who is patient — he is dear to Me.

He from whom the world does not shrink, who does not shrink from the world, who is free from exultation, anger, fear, and anxiety — he is dear to Me.

He who is skillful, indifferent to gain, free from anxiety, untroubled, who gives up all undertakings — he is dear to Me.

He who neither rejoices nor hates, neither grieves nor desires, who renounces good and evil, who is full of devotion — he is dear to Me. He who is alike to foe and friend, and also alike in honor and dishonor — he is dear to Me.

quote: Fix your mind on Me; be devoted to Me; worship Me; bow down to Me. So shall you come to Me.
body:
Fix your mind on Me; be devoted to Me; worship Me; bow down to Me. So shall you come to Me. I promise you truly, for you are dear to Me.

Abandon all varieties of dharma and simply surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.

Knowledge is better than practice; meditation is better than knowledge; but better still is surrender of attachment to results, because there follows immediate peace.

quote: Whenever virtue declines and wickedness arises, I manifest myself.
body:
Whenever virtue declines and wickedness arises, I manifest myself. To protect the righteous, to annihilate the wicked, and to reestablish the principles of dharma, I appear age after age.

Even if you are considered the most sinful of all sinners, when you are in the boat of transcendental knowledge, you will be able to cross the ocean of miseries. As the blazing fire turns firewood to ashes, the fire of knowledge burns to ashes all reactions to material activities.

There is nothing more purifying than this knowledge. In due course of time one who is perfected in yoga finds this knowledge within himself through his own realization.

quote: I am the taste of water, the light of the sun and the moon.
body:
I am the taste of water, the light of the sun and the moon, the syllable Om in the Vedic mantras; I am the sound in ether and ability in man.

I am the original fragrance of the earth, and I am the heat in fire. I am the life of all that lives, and I am the penances of all ascetics.

O son of Pritha, know that I am the original seed of all existences, the intelligence of the intelligent, and the prowess of all powerful men.

Of the mighty, I am the strength devoid of desire and passion. Of the Pandavas, I am Arjuna; of the sages, I am Vyasa; and among great thinkers, I am Usana, the sage.

quote: I am the same to all beings. There is none hateful to Me, and none dear.
body:
I am the same to all beings. There is none hateful to Me, and none dear. But those who worship Me with devotion are in Me, and I am in them.

If even the most sinful worships Me with exclusive devotion, he is to be considered righteous, for he has rightly resolved. He soon becomes righteous and attains to eternal peace; know thou for certain that My devotee is never destroyed.

Fill your mind with Me; be My devotee; worship Me; bow down to Me; so shall you come to Me. I pledge you my troth; you are dear to Me.

quote: Time I am, the great destroyer of the worlds.
body:
Time I am, the great destroyer of the worlds, and I have come here to destroy all these men. Even without you, all the warriors who are arrayed in the opposing armies shall cease to exist.

Arjuna, having heard this, trembled and, with folded hands, bowed down. He spoke: I see You blazing like a fire in ten thousand directions. You are infinite, of immeasurable strength, and You pervade everything.

O Lord of lords, so fierce of form, please tell me who You are. I offer my obeisances unto You; please be gracious to me. You are the primal God, the ancient; You are the ultimate resting place of all this universe. You are the knower and the thing to be known and the supreme abode.

quote: The field, that is the body; and he who knows the field — that is Me.
body:
The field, that is the body; and he who knows the field — that is what the wise call the knower of the field. Understand also that I am the knower in all the fields. Understanding of the field and the knower of the field — this is considered by Me to be real knowledge.

Know that which pervades the entire body is indestructible. No one is able to destroy the imperishable soul. The material body of the indestructible, immeasurable, and eternal living entity is sure to come to an end.

Humility, freedom from pride, non-violence, tolerance, simplicity, approaching a genuine spiritual master, cleanliness, steadiness, and self-control; renunciation of the objects of sense enjoyment — these are declared to be knowledge, and what is contrary to these is ignorance.

quote: He who is undeluded, knowing Me as the Supreme Personality, knows everything.
body:
There are two classes of beings in this world — the fallible and the infallible. In the material world every living entity is fallible, and in the spiritual world every living entity is called infallible.

Besides these two, there is the greatest living personality, the Supreme Soul, the imperishable Lord Himself, who has entered the three worlds and is maintaining them.

He who is undeluded, knowing Me as the Supreme Personality, knows everything and therefore engages in complete devotion to Me.

This is the most secret part of the Vedic scriptures, O sinless one, and it is disclosed now by Me. Whoever understands this will become wise, and his endeavors will know perfection.

quote: To the mind that is still, the whole universe surrenders.
body:
To the mind that is still, the whole universe surrenders.

The Tao that can be told is not the eternal Tao. The name that can be named is not the eternal name. The nameless is the beginning of heaven and earth.

Knowing others is wisdom; knowing yourself is enlightenment. Mastering others requires force; mastering yourself requires strength.

Do you have the patience to wait until your mud settles and the water is clear? Can you remain unmoving until the right action arises by itself?

quote: Flow with whatever may happen and let your mind be free.
body:
Flow with whatever may happen and let your mind be free. Stay centered by accepting whatever you are doing. This is the ultimate.

Happiness is the absence of the striving for happiness. To have no striving is to have no mind.

Only the person who has faith in himself is able to be faithful to others. If water derives lucidity from stillness, how much more the faculties of the mind.

quote: Empty your mind of all thoughts. Let your heart be at peace.
body:
Empty your mind of all thoughts. Let your heart be at peace. Watch the turmoil of beings, but contemplate their return.

Each separate being in the universe returns to the common source. Returning to the source is serenity. If you don't realize the source, you stumble in confusion and sorrow.

When you realize where you come from, you naturally become tolerant, disinterested, amused, kindhearted as a grandmother, dignified as a king. Immersed in the wonder of the Tao, you can deal with whatever life brings you, and when death comes, you are ready.

quote: The highest good is like water — it nourishes without effort.
body:
The highest good is like water. Water gives life to the ten thousand things and does not strive. It flows in places men reject and so is like the Tao.

In dwelling, live close to the ground. In thinking, keep to the simple. In conflict, be fair and generous. In governing, don't try to control. In work, do what you enjoy. In family life, be completely present.

When you are content to be simply yourself and don't compare or compete, everybody will respect you.

quote: He who knows others is wise. He who knows himself is enlightened.
body:
He who knows others is wise. He who knows himself is enlightened. He who conquers others has physical strength. He who conquers himself is strong.

He who is content is wealthy. He who perseveres has willpower. He who maintains his position endures. He who is not afraid of death will live forever.

For the Tao is great and so is heaven, and so is earth, and so is the human. These four are great, and the human is one of them. The way of humans is earthly; the way of earth is heavenly; the way of heaven is the Tao; the way of the Tao is its own nature.

quote: Yield and overcome. Bend and be straight.
body:
Yield and overcome. Bend and be straight. Empty and be full. Wear out and be new. Have little and gain. Have much and be confused.

Therefore the Sage embraces the one and becomes the model of all. He does not show himself, and therefore is luminous. He does not justify himself, and therefore becomes distinguished. He does not make claims, and therefore is acknowledged. He does not boast, and therefore advances.

It is because he does not contend that no one can contend with him. The ancient saying 'Yield and overcome' — is this an empty saying? Be truly whole and all things will come to you.

quote: Fame or self: which matters more? Self or wealth: which is more precious?
body:
Fame or self: which matters more? Self or wealth: which is more precious? Gain or loss: which is more painful?

He who is attached to things will suffer much. He who saves will suffer heavy loss. A contented man is never disappointed. He who knows when to stop does not find himself in trouble. He will stay forever safe.

The Tao is always at ease. It overcomes without competing, answers without speaking a word, arrives without being summoned, accomplishes without a plan. Its net covers the whole universe — and though its meshes are wide, it doesn't let a thing slip through.

quote: My life has a limit but my knowledge is without limit.
body:
My life has a limit, but my knowledge is without limit. To drive the limited in pursuit of the limitless is fatal; and to presume that one really knows is fatal indeed.

In striving for others, avoid fame. In striving for self, avoid disgrace. Pursue a middle course. In this way you will keep a sound body, and a sound mind, fulfil your duties, and work out your allotted span.

The cook who carved an ox did so not by brute force, but by following the natural structure of the animal. He found the hollow spaces, the great joints, and he placed his chopper there. The secret is to go along with what is natural.

quote: Nothing in the world is as soft as water, yet nothing is better at overcoming the hard and strong.
body:
Nothing in the world is as soft and yielding as water. Yet for dissolving the hard and inflexible, nothing can surpass it. The soft overcomes the hard; the gentle overcomes the rigid. Everyone knows this is true, but few can put it into practice.

Therefore the Master remains serene in the midst of sorrow. Evil cannot enter his heart. Because he has given up helping, he is people's greatest help.

True words seem paradoxical. The Tao does not force — and therefore it overcomes. The Tao does not impose — and therefore it prevails. These are the words of a sage: sometimes by giving up you gain more.

quote: The sage does not compete, and therefore no one can compete with him.
body:
True words are not beautiful; beautiful words are not true. Good men do not argue; those who argue are not good. Those who know are not learned; the learned do not know.

The sage never tries to store things up. The more he does for others, the more he has. The more he gives to others, the greater his own abundance. The Tao of heaven is pointed but does no harm. The Tao of the sage is work without effort.

The sage does not compete, and therefore no one can compete with him. Heaven's Way is to benefit and not harm; the Way of the sage is to act but not to contend.

quote: You are what your deep, driving desire is.
body:
You are what your deep, driving desire is. As your desire is, so is your will. As your will is, so is your deed. As your deed is, so is your destiny.

From joy springs all creation, by joy it is sustained, towards joy it proceeds, and into joy it enters.

This Self is never born nor does it ever perish; it did not come into existence, and it will not come into existence. This unborn, eternal, ever-existing, primordial being is not slain when the body is slain.

quote: The Self cannot be known by one who is diverted by wealth.
body:
The Self cannot be known by one who is diverted by wealth, or by one who is careless, or by one who is not at peace with oneself. But through the light of the Self, the seer sees the Self.

Brahmam is supreme; it is self-luminous, it is beyond all thought. Subtler than the subtlest is it, farther than the farthest, nearer than the nearest. It is the light in the heart.

As rivers, flowing down, become indistinguishable on reaching the sea, by giving up their names and forms, so also the illumined soul, having become freed from name and form, reaches the self-effulgent Purusha that is higher than the higher.

quote: The good and the pleasant are two different things. The wise choose the good.
body:
The good and the pleasant are two different things; they bind a person to different ends. It is well with the one who chooses the good; the one who chooses the pleasant misses the goal.

Both the good and the pleasant approach a person. Going round both, the wise discriminates and chooses the good over the pleasant. The ignorant chooses the pleasant for the sake of bodily enjoyment.

The Self is not known through discourse, nor through the intellect, nor through much learning. It is known to the one whom It chooses; to such a one, the Self reveals Its own nature.

quote: That art thou — Tat Tvam Asi.
body:
As the bees make honey by collecting the juices of distant trees, and reduce the juice into one form, and as these juices have no discrimination, so that they might say 'I am the juice of this tree or that tree,' in the same manner all these creatures, when they have become merged in the True, know not that they are merged in the True.

That which is the finest essence — this whole world has that as its soul. That is Reality. That is Atman. That art thou, O Shvetaketu.

As the rivers flowing east and west merge in the sea and become one with it, forgetting that they ever were separate rivers, so do all creatures lose their separateness when they merge at last into pure Being.

quote: Om — the past, the present, the future — everything is Om.
body:
Om — the past, the present, the future — everything is Om. And whatever transcends these three divisions of time is also Om.

All this is Brahman. The Self is Brahman. The Self has four quarters.

The waking state, outward-knowing, having seven limbs, nineteen mouths, experiencing the gross, is the first quarter. The dreaming state, inward-knowing, having seven limbs, nineteen mouths, experiencing the subtle, is the second quarter. The deep sleep state, where one does not desire any object, does not see any dream, is the third quarter.

The fourth quarter is that which is not conscious of the internal world, nor of the external world, nor of both together; not a mass of consciousness, neither conscious nor unconscious. It is unseen, unrelated, incomprehensible, uninferable, unthinkable, indescribable. It is the essence of the one self-cognition common to all states of consciousness. All phenomena cease in it. It is peace, it is bliss, it is non-duality. This is the Self. This is to be known.

quote: All this — whatever exists in this changing universe — is pervaded by God.
body:
All this — whatever exists in this changing universe — is pervaded by God. Protect yourself by detachment, and then enjoy. Do not covet what belongs to others.

Only by doing works here on earth may you desire to live a hundred years. There is no other way. Actions do not cling to a person who lives thus.

He who sees all beings in the Self and the Self in all beings, he never turns away from it. When a seer sees the brilliant Maker, Lord, Person, the Brahman-source, then being a knower, shaking off good and evil, he reaches the supreme oneness, free from passions.

quote: From joy, indeed, all these beings are born.
body:
From joy, indeed, all these beings are born; having been born, they are sustained by joy; they move towards and merge into joy at the end.

Verily, Brahman is the food, the air, and the Self. The knower of Brahman becomes Brahman. He who knows Brahman attains the highest. He who knows Brahman becomes one with Brahman.

The one who was afraid says: I am food! I am food! I am the eater of food! I am the maker of verses. I am the firstborn of the world-order. Earlier than the gods, I am the centre of immortality.

quote: He is the inner Self of all beings, hidden like a small flame in the heart.
body:
He is the inner Self of all beings, hidden like a small flame in the heart. He alone is God in all regions, born in the beginning, he is within the womb. He alone is born, and he is to be born; he stands opposite all persons with his face towards them.

He is the God who is in fire, in water, in plants, in trees. God is in all things. Salutation to that God, the Universal.

The Lord God, the omniscient, the one abode of all — having entered the cave of the heart and dwelling there — is the ruler of time past and time to come.

quote: Be still and know that I am God.
body:
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble. Therefore we will not fear, even though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea.

There is a river whose streams shall make glad the city of God, the holy place of the tabernacle of the Most High.

Be still, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.

quote: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.
body:
Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted. Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.

Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy. Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God. Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God.

You are the light of the world. A city that is set on a hill cannot be hidden. Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works.

quote: Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding.
body:
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.

Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the Lord and shun evil. This will bring health to your body and nourishment to your bones.

Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it. Keep your mouth free of perversity; keep corrupt talk far from your lips. Let your eyes look straight ahead; fix your gaze directly before you. Give careful thought to the paths for your feet and be steadfast in all your ways.

quote: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
body:
The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want. He makes me to lie down in green pastures; He leads me beside the still waters. He restores my soul; He leads me in the paths of righteousness for His name's sake.

Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil; for You are with me; Your rod and Your staff, they comfort me.

You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; You anoint my head with oil; my cup runs over. Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.

quote: We know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him.
body:
We know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.

For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall trouble or hardship or persecution or famine or nakedness or danger or sword? No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.

quote: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast.
body:
Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs.

Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.

For now we see only a reflection as in a mirror; then we shall see face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I am fully known.

And now these three remain: faith, hope and love. But the greatest of these is love.

quote: There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens.
body:
There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens: a time to be born and a time to die, a time to plant and a time to uproot, a time to kill and a time to heal, a time to tear down and a time to build.

A time to weep and a time to laugh, a time to mourn and a time to dance, a time to scatter stones and a time to gather them, a time to embrace and a time to refrain from embracing.

He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in the human heart; yet no one can fathom what God has done from beginning to end.

quote: I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content.
body:
I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content. I know both how to be abased, and I know how to abound: every where and in all things I am instructed both to be full and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer need. I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.

Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are honest, whatsoever things are just, whatsoever things are pure, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report; if there be any virtue, and if there be any praise, think on these things.

And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.

quote: Those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength.
body:
Do you not know? Have you not heard? The Lord is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He will not grow tired or weary, and his understanding no one can fathom.

He gives strength to the weary and increases the power of the weak. Even youths grow tired and weary, and young men stumble and fall; but those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength.

They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint.

quote: Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap — yet your heavenly Father feeds them.
body:
Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes?

Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they? Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life?

See how the flowers of the field grow. They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these. So do not worry, saying 'What shall we eat?' or 'What shall we drink?' or 'What shall we wear?' But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.

quote: Where were you when I laid the earth's foundation?
body:
Then the Lord spoke to Job out of the storm. He said: Who is this that obscures my plans with words without knowledge? Brace yourself like a man; I will question you, and you shall answer me.

Where were you when I laid the earth's foundation? Tell me, if you understand. Who marked off its dimensions? Surely you know! Who stretched a measuring line across it? On what were its footings set, or who laid its cornerstone — while the morning stars sang together and all the angels shouted for joy?

Have you comprehended the vast expanses of the earth? Tell me, if you know all this. What is the way to the abode of light? And where does darkness reside?

quote: Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear.
body:
Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear. It will have what it has earned, and it will be held accountable for what it has deserved.

Verily, with hardship comes ease. Verily, with hardship comes ease. So when you have finished your duties, then stand up for worship.

And seek help through patience and prayer, and indeed, it is difficult except for the humbly submissive.

quote: Do not lose heart, nor fall into despair — you will be superior if you are true in faith.
body:
Do not lose heart, nor fall into despair. For you will be superior if you are true in faith. If a wound has touched you, be sure a similar wound has touched the others. Such days of varying fortunes we give to men and men by turns.

And those who are slain in God's way, He will never let their deeds be lost. He will guide them and set right their state, and bring them into the Garden which He has made known to them.

O ye who believe! Persevere in patience and constancy; vie in such perseverance; strengthen each other; and fear God that ye may prosper.

quote: Do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins.
body:
Say: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful.

And return to your Lord and submit to Him before the punishment comes upon you; then you will not be helped. And follow the best of what was revealed to you from your Lord before the punishment comes upon you suddenly.

And whoever does a good deed — it is for himself. And whoever does evil — it is against himself. Then to your Lord you will be returned.

quote: Guide us to the straight path — the path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor.
body:
In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. All praise is due to God alone, the Sustainer of all the worlds, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful, Master of the Day of Judgment.

You alone do we worship; and unto You alone do we turn for aid. Guide us to the straight path — the path of those upon whom You have bestowed Your blessings, not of those who have been condemned, nor of those who go astray.

This is the opening chapter of the Quran, recited in every prayer. In seven short verses, it contains the essence of the entire message: gratitude, worship, and the yearning for guidance.

quote: Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent.
body:
Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent. He who created death and life to test you as to which of you is best in deed — and He is the Exalted in Might, the Forgiving.

He created seven heavens in layers. You do not see in the creation of the Most Merciful any inconsistency. So return your vision to the sky; do you see any breaks? Then return your vision twice again. Your vision will return to you humbled while it is fatigued.

All praise is to God, the originator of the heavens and the earth, the originator of all things. There is no Creator but He, so worship Him alone, and He is the Guardian of all things.

quote: The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts become fearful.
body:
The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts become fearful, and when His verses are recited to them, it increases them in faith; and upon their Lord they rely.

The ones who establish prayer, and from what We have provided them, they spend. Those are the believers, truly. For them are degrees of high position with their Lord and forgiveness and noble provision.

And know that whatever you obtain of war booty, then indeed, for Allah is one-fifth of it and for the Messenger and for near relatives and the orphans, the needy, and the stranded traveler.

quote: Then which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
body:
The Most Gracious. He taught the Quran. He created man. He taught him eloquence.

The sun and the moon move by precise calculation. And the stars and trees prostrate. And the heaven He raised and imposed the balance — that you not transgress within the balance. And establish weight in justice and do not make deficient the balance.

And the earth He laid out for creatures. Therein is fruit and palm trees having sheaths. And grain having husks and fragrant plants.

So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny? He created man from clay like pottery. And He created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire. So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?

quote: Verily, with every difficulty there is relief.
body:
Did We not expand for you your breast? And We removed from you your burden which had weighed upon your back. And raised high for you your repute.

For indeed, with hardship will be ease. Indeed, with hardship will be ease.

So when you have finished your duties, then stand up for worship. And to your Lord direct your longing.

This surah was revealed as a comfort to the Prophet in times of hardship, and its promise is universal: after every difficulty, ease follows. Not once but twice the Quran says it — as if to make certain the heart hears and believes.

quote: The mind is everything. What you think, you become.
body:
The mind is everything. What you think, you become. Mind is the forerunner of all actions. All deeds are led by mind, created by mind.

Hatred is never appeased by hatred in this world. By non-hatred alone is hatred appeased. This is a law eternal.

Better than a thousand hollow words, is one word that brings peace. Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment.

quote: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.
body:
Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor. Smile, breathe, and go slowly.

The present moment is the only moment available to us, and it is the door to all moments. People usually consider walking on water or in thin air a miracle. But I think the real miracle is to walk on earth.

The most precious gift we can offer anyone is our attention. When mindfulness embraces those we love, they will bloom like flowers.

quote: Long is the night to him who is awake.
body:
Long is the night to him who is awake; long is a mile to him who is tired; long is life to the foolish who do not know the true law.

If a traveler does not meet with one who is his better, or his equal, let him firmly keep to his solitary journey; there is no companionship with a fool.

From attachment springs grief, from attachment springs fear. For one who is wholly free from attachment there is no grief, whence then fear? From craving springs grief, from craving springs fear. For one who is free from craving there is no grief; whence then fear?

quote: Nothing ever goes away until it has taught us what we need to know.
body:
Nothing ever goes away until it has taught us what we need to know. If we run from our feelings, if we run from our fears, if we run from our discomfort, we run from our life.

The things that drive us crazy, the things that upset us — those are the very things which, if we can stay with them, soften us and wake us up. We don't have to be perfect. The point is to be real.

Fear is a natural reaction to moving closer to the truth. The dharma path is nothing more than learning to be fully present with our lives — with the joy, the sorrow, the beauty, and the ordinary.

quote: Health is the highest gain. Contentment is the highest wealth.
body:
Health is the highest gain. Contentment is the highest wealth. A trusted friend is the best relative. Nibbana is the highest happiness.

By oneself is evil done; by oneself one suffers. By oneself evil is left undone; by oneself one is purified. Purity and impurity depend on oneself. No one can purify another.

Let no one neglect one's own good for the sake of another's, however great. Clearly understanding one's own goal, let one be intent upon one's own purpose.

quote: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities, but in the expert's mind there are few.
body:
In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities, but in the expert's mind there are few. This is the most difficult thing: to always keep your beginner's mind. In the beginner's mind there is no thought 'I have attained something.'

When we have no thought of achievement, no thought of self, we are true beginners. Then we can really learn something. The beginner's mind is the mind of compassion.

When our mind is compassionate, it is boundless. In the beginner's mind, we have many possibilities. In the expert's mind, there are few. So try to maintain always a beginner's mind.

quote: I call him a brahmin who is free from anger.
body:
I call him a brahmin who is free from anger, who is careful in his behaviour, who is virtuous and free from conceit, who is controlled and who has crossed this last existence.

I call him a brahmin from whom there is no clinging as water does not cling to a lotus petal or a mustard seed on the point of a needle.

I call him a brahmin who has traversed this miry road of rebirths and delusion, difficult to cross, who has crossed it and reached the other shore, who is meditative, not agitated, not doubting and clinging to nothing, who has arrived at Nibbana by not clinging.

quote: If you let go a little, you will have a little peace.
body:
If you let go a little, you will have a little peace. If you let go a lot, you will have a lot of peace. If you let go completely, you will have complete peace.

Do not try to become anything. Do not make yourself into anything. Do not be a meditator. Do not become enlightened. When you sit, let it be. When you walk, let it be. Grasp at nothing. Resist nothing.

The heart of the path is quite simple. No great elaboration is necessary. Give up clinging to love and hate, just rest with things as they are.

quote: Better than a thousand hollow words is one word that brings peace.
body:
Better than a thousand hollow words is one word that brings peace. Better than a thousand hollow verses is one verse that brings peace. Better than a hundred hollow stanzas is one stanza that brings peace.

Though one may conquer a million men in battle, the one who conquers himself is the greatest warrior.

Better it is to live one day seeing the rise and fall of all things than to live a hundred years without ever seeing the rise and fall of all things. Better it is to live one day full of virtue than to live a hundred years wicked and unrestrained.

work: The Dalai Lama — The Art of Happiness
quote: The purpose of our existence is to seek happiness.
body:
The purpose of our existence is to seek happiness. Whether one believes in religion or not, whether one believes in this religion or that religion, we all are seeking something better in life. So, I think, the very motion of our life is towards happiness.

Happiness is determined more by one's state of mind than by external conditions, circumstances, or events. A positive state of mind is not merely good for you; it benefits everyone with whom you come into contact, literally changing the world.

If you want others to be happy, practice compassion. If you want to be happy, practice compassion.
